era_context: Late Bronze Age, mid-second millennium BCE. Thutmose I is ruling Egypt. The Hittite Old Kingdom is consolidating power in Anatolia. Mycenaean Greece is in the Late Helladic II period. The Shang Dynasty is active in China. This is a period of palace-based states, long-distance trade, and increasing military organization.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most regions outside Egypt and the Hittite heartland
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are attested for this year in any known source.
historiographic_note: This year, like -1507, has no documented events in surviving sources. The historical record for this period is extremely limited. Year-by-year precision is not achievable for most of the world in the mid-second millennium BCE. The surviving written record is concentrated in Egypt and the Hittite heartland; even there, most years are not individually documented. Archaeological evidence provides some information about settlement patterns, trade, and technological change, but rarely allows dating to specific years.
